video_summary: This video explores the evolution of software deployment, from manual processes to automated CI/CD pipelines, discussing key tools like Jenkins and GitHub Actions, and detailing various deployment strategies including manual approval, continuous delivery, and progressive rollouts.

concepts.colloquial_context: कोई नहीं फीचर इंपलमेंट करेंगे, सब से पहले तो उसके लिए हमें कोड लिखना पडगेगा, उस कोड को हम एकसिस्टिं कोड भेश के साथ मरज करेंगे, तेस्ट करेंगे आं दें दिपलोई करेंगे
concepts.description: The sequential process of developing software, which typically involves writing new code, merging it with the existing codebase, thorough testing, and finally deploying the application to users.

concepts.colloquial_context: any good company is building any scalable application it will be built within one go over time, we will have to change a lot of things within that application it can be bug fixes, future implementations
concepts.description: An application designed to handle increasing workloads or user bases over time, necessitating continuous changes, bug fixes, and feature additions rather than a one-time build.
The complete collection of source code files that comprise a particular software application or project.
The process of evaluating software to identify bugs, errors, or any deviations from expected behavior, ensuring quality before deployment.
The process of making a software application available for use by end-users, involving tasks like releasing, configuring, and enabling the software on servers.
A set of practices in software development that automates the integration, testing, and deployment of code changes, aiming to deliver software reliably and frequently.

concepts.colloquial_context: पुरा कपुरा process manual होता था. तो जब भी software development के अंदर कुछ मेंनूल होटा था वहांपर हैं, बहुत सब हैं एरर्ज होते हैं कुकी जब भी humans होते हैं, they tend to create errors.
concepts.description: The traditional method where development, testing, and deployment steps are performed manually, often leading to increased human error, inconsistencies, and slower release cycles.
Defects or flaws in software that cause it to behave unexpectedly or incorrectly, often leading to malfunctions or crashes.
Software platforms designed to automate the various stages of a CI/CD pipeline, including building, testing, and deploying applications.

An open-source automation server widely used for CI/CD, acting as a jobs orchestrator to automate software building, testing, and deployment. It's known for its extensibility but can be complex to maintain.
A feature from GitHub that allows automation of software workflows, including CI/CD, directly within a GitHub repository, often seen as a modern alternative to Jenkins.
A system or tool responsible for scheduling, managing, and executing various automated tasks or 'jobs' in a defined sequence, common in CI/CD pipelines.

concepts.colloquial_context: मैनूल अप्रुवल मिलताए अफ वराद़े लिए है, तो पुरक पुरो प्रुवल होटाए
concepts.description: A step in the deployment process where human authorization is required before a software change can be released, typically used for critical systems or highly sensitive changes.

concepts.colloquial_context: कंटिनियाज़ दिलगरी विल भी बेटर सुटटड
concepts.description: A software engineering approach where teams produce software in short cycles, ensuring that the software can be released reliably at any time, though actual deployment to production may still require a manual trigger or approval.

concepts.colloquial_context: new version की तरब लिएड़्िक करतें जेसे फर एक खरुचं की आपनी 100 यूजिं की देपल एक खाएं। तो 100 में से यनिष्ली 5-10% वूँस्छस कोई आम एक गंडटे डो गंडटे तीन आस के लेई new version की तरभ लिएड़्िक करेंगे If everything is working fine �鬼बशाक मढ़ा � ECOा ऒ़ीयी और रोधि तो लोगी कोंी बहुट और रो� only p squid After that, we will redirect more of users
concepts.description: A deployment strategy that releases a new version of software to a small subset of users first. If the new version performs well without issues, it's gradually rolled out to more users, minimizing risk.
